steps.title: Focus on fading excitement
steps.content: The underlined claim says product announcements can increase excitement, but that effect fades quickly. Supporting evidence must connect a long delay after announcement with lower excitement.

steps.title: Use timing evidence
steps.content: Survey results showing low excitement for products announced long before release directly support the timing claim. A is about new versus old products, B is about brand loyalty, and D is about the kind of information shared. The answer is C.
